county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1776; became part of WV at statehood 1863
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Monongalia County Clerk (Morgantown). Major county formed 1776 from the District of West Augusta (VA). One of WV's original counties. West Virginia University is in Morgantown -- WVU West Virginia and Regional History Center is a premier research facility with extensive manuscript collections, oral histories, and county records. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Good surviving records. Monongalia County Historical Society. Glass and coal industries. Early settlement from Pennsylvania and Maryland.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1799; became part of WV at statehood 1863
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Monroe County Clerk (Union). Formed 1799 from Greenbrier. Mountain county with good surviving records. German and Scots-Irish settlement. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Monroe County Historical Society. Civil War activity at Union and Salt Sulphur Springs. Sweet Springs and Salt Sulphur Springs resort records. Enslaved population documented in tax lists. Check Greenbrier for pre-1799 records. Agricultural county with strong livestock heritage.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1820; became part of WV at statehood 1863; Eastern Panhandle
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Morgan County Clerk (Berkeley Springs). Eastern Panhandle county formed 1820 from Hampshire and Berkeley. Berkeley Springs (Bath) is one of America's oldest spa towns -- George Washington visited. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Morgan County Historical and Genealogical Society. German and Scots-Irish settlement. Check Hampshire and Berkeley for pre-1820 records. Good surviving records. C&O Canal and Potomac River connections to Maryland records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1818; became part of WV at statehood 1863; coal mining region
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Nicholas County Clerk (Summersville). Formed 1818 from Greenbrier, Kanawha, and Randolph. Summersville Lake area -- Army Corps of Engineers relocation records. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Nicholas County Historical and Genealogical Society. Civil War battle at Carnifex Ferry (1861). Coal and timber industries important. Check parent counties for earlier records. Good surviving records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1776; became part of WV at statehood 1863; Northern Panhandle
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Ohio County Clerk (Wheeling). One of WV's two original counties (1776), formed from the District of West Augusta (VA). Wheeling was WV's first state capital (1863-1870, 1875-1885). Major Ohio River industrial city -- steel, glass, tobacco, and iron manufacturing. Ohio County Public Library has excellent genealogical collections. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Good surviving records from 1776. German, Irish, and Eastern European immigration documented. Wheeling National Cemetery records at NARA. Fort Henry and other frontier fort records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1788; became part of WV at statehood 1863
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Pendleton County Clerk (Franklin). Mountain county formed 1788 from Augusta (VA), Hardy, and Rockingham (VA). One of WV's least populous counties. German settlement from 1740s -- Seneca Rocks and North Fork area families. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond; check Virginia parent counties. Pendleton County Historical Society. Timber industry important. Spruce Knob (WV's highest point) area. Civil War activity. Enslaved population documented. Good surviving records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1851; became part of WV at statehood 1863; Ohio River county
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Pleasants County Clerk (St. Marys). Formed 1851 from Ritchie, Tyler, and Wood. Small Ohio River county. Oil and gas industry from the 1860s. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Pleasants County Historical Society. Check parent counties for earlier records. Good surviving records. Middle Island Creek area families.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1821; became part of WV at statehood 1863
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Pocahontas County Clerk (Marlinton). Mountain county formed 1821 from Bath (VA), Pendleton, and Randolph. Major timber county -- Cass Scenic Railroad documents logging history. Monongahela National Forest covers much of the county. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Pocahontas County Historical Society. German and Scots-Irish settlement. Check parent counties (including Bath in VA) for earlier records. Pearl S. Buck birthplace in Hillsboro. Extremely rural -- family networks well documented.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1818; became part of WV at statehood 1863
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Preston County Clerk (Kingwood). Formed 1818 from Monongalia. Buckwheat Festival county. Coal and timber industries. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Preston County Historical Society. Good surviving records. Check Monongalia for pre-1818 records. Cranesville Swamp area. WVU Regional History Center has Preston County materials. German and Scots-Irish settlement patterns. B&O Railroad connections.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1848; became part of WV at statehood 1863
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Putnam County Clerk (Winfield). Formed 1848 from Kanawha, Cabell, and Mason. Winfield area near Charleston. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Putnam County Historical Society. Good surviving records. Check parent counties for earlier records. Hurricane and Teays Valley area development. Chemical industry along Kanawha River.
